notes: HQ: 2001 Logic Drive, San Jose CA 95124. Private company reborn 2002 after spin-out from Quantum. Number one volume NAS provider by 2003.
Former parent of Snap Appliance; acquired Snap Servers from Meridian Data in 2000; divested as private company in 2002.
Original developer and launcher of first Snap Servers in 1998; acquired by Quantum in 2000.
Acquirer of Snap Appliance in 2008; later merged with Sphere 3D in 2014. Outcome verified per prescience context.
Publishing organization; Aberdeen's midline storage research directly cited in this profile.
Author of the SnapShot; Aberdeen co-founder; originator of midline storage taxonomy referenced in the profile.
President and CEO of Snap Appliance at time of profile.
VP Marketing and Business Development at Snap Appliance.
VP Worldwide Sales and Channel Marketing at Snap Appliance.
Chief Financial Officer at Snap Appliance.
Backup software partner of Snap Appliance; acquired by EMC around time of this profile.
Software partnership with Snap Appliance; BackupExec and NetBackup product lines.
